name: UI vs UX (clarifier)
description: Quick rubric for teams: UX vs UI responsibilities, overlap zones, and how to avoid handoff confusion.

metadata.intent: Clarify UX vs UI responsibilities so specs and feedback are actionable

must_not: Treat UI polish as a substitute for broken usability
conventions: Name what you’re asking for: flow/IA vs visuals/micro-interactions.

steps.description: UX design = shaping the end-to-end experience: research, user journeys, IA, interaction design, testing, iteration. Success is: users achieve goals with minimal friction.
UI design = visual and interactive layer: layout, typography, color tokens, components, motion, states. Success is: clarity, accessibility, aesthetic coherence, predictable behavior.
Overlap = product design: both disciplines share user-centered thinking and collaboration with engineering. A strong UI supports UX; it doesn’t replace it.
